title: Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences Institutional
content:
Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences Institutional

1. About Us

What is Engineering and Natural Sciences?

As a thematic university in the field of behavioral health and sciences, Üsküdar University’s Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences aims to conduct interdisciplinary research and studies, benefiting from the 16 years of experience of NPİSTANBUL Brain Hospital.

Undergraduate Programs in Engineering and Natural Sciences

The Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences consists of nine departments:

Forensic Science (Turkish)

Computer Engineering (English)

Bioengineering (English)

Electrical and Electronics Engineering (English)

Industrial Engineering (English)

Chemical Engineering (English)

Molecular Biology and Genetics (English)

Molecular Biology and Genetics (Turkish)

Software Engineering (English)

Employment Opportunities For Graduates Of The Faculty Of Engineering And Natural Sciences

Forensic Science Graduates

Graduates of the Forensic Science undergraduate program can work in the central and provincial units of the Criminal Police Laboratories Department of the General Directorate of Security under the Ministry of Interior, including the Criminal Research and Technical Investigation Training Branch (KATEM), Research and Development Branch, Substance Abuse Bureau, Crime Scene Investigation and Identification Branch, Narcotics Branch, and Interpol Branch. They may also serve in the Gendarmerie General Command’s Criminal Department and Regional Forensic Laboratories, as well as in provincial and district crime scene investigation teams, Training and Scientific Research and Support Units of the Ministry of Justice’s Council of Forensic Medicine (ATK), in the Physics, Biology, and Chemistry departments of the central organization, or in regional and branch-level laboratories.

Computer Engineering Graduates

Computer Engineering graduates can work in diverse fields such as health informatics, software development, robotics, systems engineering, database administration, R&D, digital forensics and cybersecurity, hardware/electronics, web design and programming, and multimedia. They also have opportunities to pursue academic careers.

Bioengineering Graduates

Bioengineering graduates can take part in production, research, and development activities across various industries, including pharmaceuticals, medical devices, genetics, biotechnology, energy, agricultural innovation, and environmental remediation. They are also well-positioned to continue in academic research and higher education.

Electrical and Electronics Engineering Graduates

Electrical and Electronics Engineering covers a wide range of fields such as electronic circuits, biomedical systems, energy systems (generation, transmission, and distribution), control systems, communications, automation, computing, and information storage and transmission.

Modern technologies like mobile communication, e-commerce, computers, and the internet are built upon electrical and electronics engineering foundations.

Its importance will continue to grow with emerging technologies such as smart homes, smart roads, and smart cities—all of which rely on electrical and electronic systems. Automation systems and robotics used across industries are also key application areas. In summary, electrical and electronics engineers not only build the infrastructure of the information age but also solve complex problems across other scientific and engineering disciplines.

Industrial Engineering Graduates

Industrial Engineering graduates can work in both public and private organizations producing goods or services, offering a broad spectrum of career opportunities. They focus on increasing efficiency, optimizing resource utilization, and designing products, processes, and systems that best meet organizational needs. In academic institutions, they contribute to scientific progress through their research and projects.

Chemical Engineering Graduates

Graduates of Üsküdar University’s Chemical Engineering (English) Department have a wide range of career opportunities. Chemical engineers can work in production, planning,

marketing, and R&D departments of industries involving chemical processes and facilities. They can find employment in sectors such as petrochemicals, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, energy, cement, paint, plastics, biotechnology, agriculture, metals, paper, food, and textiles. Chemical engineers may work in both public and private sectors and can also pursue academic careers in Turkey or abroad with the support of various research grants.

Molecular Biology and Genetics Graduates

Graduates of the Molecular Biology and Genetics program can pursue academic careers in Turkey or abroad with the support of research grants. In addition, they can work in biotechnology companies, the pharmaceutical industry, public and private institutions in environmental and health fields, hospitals, IVF centers, diagnostic laboratories, and biomedical device companies.

Software Engineering Graduates

Software Engineering graduates have career opportunities in computer systems and software, mobile application development, web-based systems, and embedded system design and production. They may also work in healthcare engineering applications or develop AI-based solutions for industrial firms, offering versatile career paths in both industry and research.

2. Faculty Administrative Structure And Functioning

Established in 2012, the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences at Üsküdar University aims to cultivate young engineers and qualified scientists who can transform knowledge into technology and contribute to societal benefit.

Our students benefit from the advantages of our campus, located in the heart of Üsküdar and within easy reach of various transportation hubs. Academically, they have the opportunity to participate in numerous congresses, symposiums, conferences, and workshops organized within the university. Socially, they can join more than 60 student clubs and sports teams, enhancing their university experience.

Through various interdisciplinary activities, students can engage in cross-disciplinary studies, design personalized academic programs by choosing elective courses, and benefit from double major and minor degree opportunities.

Our education programs are fully compatible with the European Credit Transfer System (ECTS). Students are encouraged to participate in international exchange programs and engage in national or international social responsibility projects.

The curricula of our nine departments, which offer programs in both English and Turkish, are designed by considering not only the professional requirements but also the future needs of society. Theoretical knowledge is reinforced through practical applications in modern laboratories.

Under the guidance of a distinguished academic staff, each program within the Faculty of Engineering and Natural Sciences provides education at international standards. Graduates of the Molecular Biology and Genetics, Bioengineering, Computer Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Software Engineering, Forensic Science, Chemical Engineering, and Electrical and Electronics Engineering programs contribute significantly to the country’s scientific foundation and skilled workforce.

Our faculty collaborates with both domestic and international universities, as well as public and private institutions, developing joint projects and organizing technical visits and workshops to enrich students’ learning experiences.

We also host international students, creating a diverse and multicultural learning environment that fosters the exchange of ideas and perspectives. This international atmosphere, combined with our accredited programs, enables our graduates to pursue careers and further education opportunities anywhere in the world.
